issues.issue: Choice-1 (self-introduction) has low narrative weight relative to setup — all three options funnel to identical scene-lunch-setup with only flavor differences
issues.impact: First choice feels cosmetic, slightly undercutting the promise that 'every choice matters' right at the tutorial moment
issues.fix: Let choice-1 subtly tag a trait that a later scene (e.g. Alex or Maya) explicitly references, so payoff is visible later

issues.issue: Choice-4 (project roles) is the only branch point that funnels all three options into an identical next scene without any visible dialogue divergence carried forward except via responseDialogue
issues.impact: Players may feel the 'take charge vs collaborate vs defer' decision doesn't ripple into the climax scene itself, only into throwaway reaction lines
issues.fix: Have Mr. Alvarez or Maya reference the chosen approach explicitly in scene-climax dialogue to reinforce continuity

issues.issue: Branch-popular-lonely path has the least dialogue depth and no character (Jordan) presence at the ending, making it feel like an afterthought compared to the other three endings
issues.impact: Players who chose the 'popular' path across the whole episode get a thinner payoff scene than other paths, reducing incentive to explore that route fully
issues.fix: Add 1-2 more lines showing the specific cost (e.g., a beat with Jordan noticing the shift) to match the emotional density of other endings

issues.issue: Six scenes in a row (lunch-setup through project-team) are single-location cafeteria/classroom conversation beats with similar rhythm — no scene changes location or activity type
issues.impact: Risk of monotony in the middle stretch since all scenes are seated dialogue exchanges with similar visual/spatial framing
issues.fix: Vary staging within scenes (e.g., a hallway beat, walking to class) to break up the visual sameness even if the underlying choice structure stays similar

issues.issue: No explicit trait-change feedback is shown to the player within the dialogue (e.g., a subtle UI/text cue confirming CONFIDENCE or EMPATHY shifted)
issues.impact: Players may not learn the CONFIDENCE/EMPATHY/ADAPTABILITY system is being tracked, reducing strategic replay motivation
issues.fix: Add lightweight non-intrusive trait feedback (even just an INTERNAL line hinting at the shift) after key choices like choice-3a/b/c and choice-5

strengths: Strong, efficient opening hook — establishes stakes (fourth first day) and voice within 7 lines
Choice-2 (lunch seating) is an excellent central dilemma: no objectively correct answer, clear value expression, immediately visible consequences

Climax choice (choice-5) is genuinely the best-designed decision point in the episode — four distinct options with nuanced trade-offs (diplomatic advocacy vs. peace-keeping vs. firm loyalty vs. creative synthesis), avoiding a binary morality trap
Good branching architecture: three lunch paths (popular/Alex/bridge) each lead to three sub-choices, creating genuine structural variety rather than choices that just reconverge instantly
Character reactivity is consistent — Maya, Alex, and Jordan visibly react differently based on prior choices, giving strong impact visibility
Four distinct, well-differentiated endings with different tonal notes (bittersweet, quietly earned, small-but-real, integrative) support replayability
Choice frequency is well-distributed roughly every 1-2 scenes, avoiding long passive stretches
recommendations: Add a brief mid-episode moment (e.g., between classroom and lunch) that lets the player see a small consequence of choice-1 to build trust in the choice-consequence loop early
Consider a light 'relationship meter' or subtle recurring visual motif (e.g., Alex's notebook, Maya's eyebrow) that changes based on trust built, to reinforce agency without breaking immersion
Vary scene staging/blocking descriptions to avoid a run of visually similar 'sitting at a table talking' scenes
Ensure the ending reflecting the 'popular' path gets a comparable emotional beat count to the others

summary.verdict: A well-constructed branching episode with a strong hook, a genuinely excellent climax choice, and satisfying variety across four distinct endings. Minor pacing sameness in the middle stretch and a slightly underweight first choice keep it from top marks, but nothing here blocks a good player experience.
keyIssues: Middle scenes share similar staging/rhythm, risking mild monotony
First choice (self-intro) has low visible consequence weight
Popular-path ending is thinner than the other three
topStrengths: Climax choice-5 is a standout, nuanced four-way dilemma
Lunch-seating choice is a strong, value-expressive fork with real downstream branching
Distinct, well-earned multiple endings support replay
niceToHave: Vary scene blocking/location in the middle act
